core.title: party
core.description: person or organization

core.title: agent
core.description: something autonomous that can adapt to and interact with its environment

core.title: person
core.description: individual human being, with consciousness of self

core.title: legally competent natural person
core.description: person who is considered competent, under the circumstances, to enter into a contract, conduct business, or participate in other activities that generally require the mental ability to understand problems and make decisions on his or her own behalf

core.title: customer
core.description: party that receives or consumes products (goods or services) and has the ability to choose between different products and suppliers

core.title: client
core.description: party that purchases professional services from, or has a formal relationship to purchase services from another party

core.title: contract party
core.description: legally competent party that has entered into a binding agreement, accepting and conceding obligations, responsibilities, and benefits as specified

core.title: person name
core.description: designation by which someone is known in some context

core.title: account holder
core.description: party that owns an account

core.title: customer account
core.description: account that represents an identified, named collection of balances and cumulative totals used to summarize customer transaction-related activity over a designated period of time

core.title: customer account holder
core.description: party that owns a customer account

core.title: legal entity
core.description: legal person that is a partnership, corporation, or other organization having the capacity to negotiate contracts, assume financial obligations, and pay off debts, organized under the laws of some jurisdiction

core.title: legal person
core.description: party that is recognized as having rights and obligations under the law, including but not limited to the right to sue and be sued, enter into contracts, own property, and incur financial and other obligations

core.title: formal organization
core.description: organization that is recognized in some legal jurisdiction, with associated rights and responsibilities

core.title: organization
core.description: framework of authority within which a person, persons, or groups of people act, or are designated to act, towards some purpose, such as to meet a need or pursue collective goals

core.title: corporation
core.description: formal organization that is a legal entity (artificial person) distinct from its owners, created under the jurisdiction of the laws of a state or nation

core.title: for profit corporation
core.description: corporation whose objective is to make money, i.e., to ensure realization of a financial benefit such that the amount of revenue gained from a business activity exceeds the expenses, costs and taxes needed to sustain that activity

core.title: not-for-profit corporation
core.description: corporation approved by its jurisdictional oversight and tax authorities as operating for educational, charitable, social, religious, civic or humanitarian purposes

core.title: chartered legal person
core.description: a legal person created by a royal charter or decree

core.title: LEI registered entity
core.description: a legal person that has registered for and is identified by a legal entity identifier

core.title: non-governmental organization
core.description: not-for-profit organization that functions independently of government

core.title: not for profit organization
core.description: organization that uses its surplus revenues to further achieve its purpose rather than distributing its surplus income to the organization's owners (directors, investors, and equivalents) as profit / dividends

core.title: partnership
core.description: association of two or more legal persons to carry on as co-owners a business for profit

core.title: general partnership
core.description: partnership that has at least two general partners that agree to share in all assets, profits, and financial and legal liabilities of the business

core.title: limited partnership
core.description: partnership that has at least one general partner and at least one limited partner

core.title: limited liability partnership
core.description: partnership that has general partners but provides its individual partners some level of protection against personal liability for certain partnership liabilities

core.title: trust
core.description: fiduciary relationship and legal entity in which one party, known as a trustor, gives another party, the trustee, the right to hold title to and manage assets for the benefit of a third party, the beneficiary

core.title: living trust
core.description: trust created during an individual's lifetime where a designated person, the trustee, is given responsibility for managing that individual's assets for the benefit of the eventual beneficiary

core.title: irrevocable trust
core.description: trust that cannot be modified, amended or terminated except under certain legal circumstances and typically not without the permission of the grantor's named beneficiary or beneficiaries

core.title: ownership
core.description: situation in which some party holds the legal title to something (explicitly or implicitly) and has the right to transfer that title and/or possession

core.title: entity ownership
core.description: ownership by some party of an interest in some non-governmental formal organization

core.title: ownership control situation
core.description: situation in which some party owns and controls something

core.title: beneficial owner
core.description: party that enjoys the benefits of ownership (such as receipt of income) of something even though its ownership (title) may be in the name of another party (called a nominee or registered owner)

core.title: beneficial ownership
core.description: situation linking party that enjoys the benefits of ownership (such as receipt of income) of something even though its ownership (title) may be in the name of another party (called a nominee or registered owner) to the asset that they own

core.title: control
core.description: situation in which some party has the power to direct or strongly influence the direction of the management and policies related to something

core.title: de facto control
core.description: control that exists informally and is accepted, although not formally recognized

core.title: de jure control
core.description: control that exists as a matter of law, i.e., legitimate, legal control of something

core.title: controlling party
core.description: actor that exercises some form of control in the context of some situation

core.title: entity controlling party
core.description: party that has the authority to control some legal entity

core.title: controlled party
core.description: person or organization over which some party exercises some form of authority or influence with respect to some situation

core.title: majority controlling party
core.description: controlling party that possesses, either directly or indirectly, the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of a legal person, whether through the ownership of a majority of voting securities, by contract, or otherwise

core.title: controlling equity
core.description: shareholders's equity that formally confers control in the entity, either by law or as explicitly stated in a corresponding equity instrument

core.title: board composition control
core.description: situation in which a voting shareholder, entity owner, or some other party in the case of a not-for-profit organization, appoints and/or nominates someone to the board of directors of an organization for some period of time

core.title: de facto controlling interest party
core.description: party that exercises some control over an entity other than via explicit, legal means

core.title: de jure controlling interest party
core.description: party that has the legal authority to exercise control

core.title: contractual control
core.description: control established through the terms of some contract

core.title: legal entity identifier
core.description: an organization identifier that uniquely identifies a legal person as defined in ISO 17442

core.title: legal entity identifier scheme
core.description: a scheme that specifies the elements of an unambiguous legal entity identifier (LEI) scheme to identify the legal entities relevant to any financial transaction

core.title: entity legal form identifier
core.description: code that denotes an entity legal form as defined in ISO 20275

core.title: business identifier code
core.description: international identifier for financial and non-financial institutions used to facilitate automated processing of information for financial services

core.title: identity document
core.description: any legal document which may be used to verify aspects of a person's identity

core.title: driver's license identifier
core.description: identifier associated with a drivers' or operating license for operating a motor vehicle or non-driver identification card

core.title: customer identifier
core.description: sequence of characters uniquely identifying a customer within the context of some organization

core.title: client identifier
core.description: sequence of characters uniquely identifying a client within the context of some organization

core.title: business registration authority
core.description: registration authority that is responsible for maintaining a registry of business entities

core.title: legal entity identifier registry
core.description: registry for registering and maintaining information about business entities for a particular jurisdiction

core.title: business register identifier
core.description: identifier that uniquely identifies a business register, such as a register identified by the Global Legal Entity Identifier Foundation (GLEIF) registration authorities list

core.title: registration authority code
core.description: identifier that uniquely identifies a business registry, and is associated with a registration authority and jurisdiction, issued by the Global Legal Entity Identifier Foundation (GLEIF)
